design_philosophy.vibe: Objective, rigid, functional. The interface should feel like an elite command center or a high-end code editor.
rules: 95% monochrome interface. Color exists ONLY to guide action (e.g., Red for timer/errors, Green for pass/success).
Control Room Grid: Dense, compact spacing for the test interface to maximize code and question visibility.
No full-screen gradients. Use solid stark colors and 1px borders to define structure.
Optical clarity over decorative styling. Remove unnecessary shadows and rounded corners.

layout_and_spacing.gap_strategy: gap-4 or gap-6 for tight developer tools density
layout_and_spacing.grid_structure: Grid Borders (The 'Technical' Look). Use pure border-collapse style layout for the assessment screen. Example: Sidebar 20%, Question Prompt 40%, Code Editor 40%. All separated by stark 1px borders.

components.buttons: Flat, sharp corners (rounded-none). Primary is black with white text. Hover states invert or translate slightly. Always set explicit hover text color.
components.inputs: Flat, 1px border border-slate-300 bg-transparent rounded-none. Focus state: ring-2 ring-slate-950 outline-none.
components.cards: border border-slate-200 bg-white shadow-none rounded-none. High-contrast text.
components.code_editor: Use JetBrains Mono. The code editor pane should have a dark background (#0A0A0A) with light text to mimic professional IDEs and create stark contrast against the light theme UI.
components.timer: Monospace font (JetBrains Mono), text-2xl font-bold. Turns red (text-red-500) when under 5 minutes.
components.question_navigator: Grid of small square buttons (rounded-none) inside a sidebar. Completed = Black fill/White text, Current = Ringed/Bordered, Unanswered = Gray 100 fill.
components.tables: Used for Results history. Dense padding, 1px borders between rows, uppercase headers with tight tracking.

images.description: White concrete building facade pattern - minimal geometric structure.
Monitor showing programming code, used for landing page context or placeholders.

icons.style: Minimal, 1.5px stroke weight, sharp, no fill unless active.

motion_and_interactions.hover: Explicit text color changes on hover (e.g., hover:bg-slate-900 hover:text-white). Use 1px sharp borders for hover interactions.
motion_and_interactions.transitions: Fast and snappy. `transition-all duration-150 ease-out`. Avoid sluggish animations in developer tools to prioritize perceived performance.

testing_attributes.rule: All interactive elements MUST include data-testid attributes.

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the high-contrast monochromatic Swiss design. Avoid rounded corners; override all Shadcn components to use `rounded-none` for a sharp, technical aesthetic.
Ensure all generated components use .js (React/JSX) and not .tsx files.
The test screen layout should be dense: Sidebar on the left for navigation, Question prompt in the top-middle, Code editor in the bottom-middle, and Output panel on the right or below the editor.
Use JetBrains Mono for all code inputs, outputs, and the timer.
The header must be highly visible and sticky with z-50.
Do NOT use placeholder images from dummyimage.com. Use the provided Unsplash URLs.
Apply `data-testid` to all forms, buttons, and navigation elements.
Implement Shadcn UI but remove default soft shadows (`shadow-none`) to maintain flat, brutalist clarity.
Add 'lucide-react' for icons if not already present in package.json.
